COURT (JUDGE MAHONEY), HAVING TAKEN THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION, NOW ISSUES AN "ORDER STAYING THE WRIT OF EXECUTION AND ORDERING FURTHER BRIEFING".

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-DEFENDANT GARY ROSSI'S MOTION TO RECALL AND QUASH WRIT OF EXECUTION AND TO ENFORCE THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: THE MOTION IS DENIED. ALTHOUGH THE COURT HAS INHERENT POWER UNDER MEYER V MEYER (1952) 115 CAL. APP. 2D 48 TO QUASH A WRIT, THE EXERCISE OF SUCH POWER WOULD REQUIRE THE COURT TO ANALYZE THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT TO DETERMINE WHETHER THERE WAS A BREACH. AS A CONSEQUENCE, IT IS THE COURT'S CONCLUSION THAT C.C.P. SECTION 664.6 CONTROLS AND LACKS JURISDICTION TO DECIDE THIS ISSUE. THIS DOES NOT PRECLUDE ROSSI FROM FILING A SEPARATE ACTION TO ENFORCE THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

MINI MINUTES: CROSS-DEFENDANT GARY ROSSI'S MOTION TO RECALL AND QUASH WRIT OF EXECUTION AND TO ENFORCE THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT.
APPEARANCES: DAVID P. WASSERMAN, ESQUIRE (WASSERMAN-STERN, 415.567.9600) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. PAUL F. UTRECHT, ESQUIRE (ZACKS UTRECHT & LEADBETTER, 415.956.8100) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: THE MOTION IS DENIED. ALTHOUGH THE COURT HAS INHERENT POWER UNDER MEYER V MEYER (1952) 115 CAL. APP. 2D 48 TO QUASH A WRIT, THE EXERCISE OF SUCH POWER WOULD REQUIRE THE COURT TO ANALYZE THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT TO DETERMINE WHETHER THERE WAS A BREACH. AS A CONSEQUENCE, IT IS THE COURT'S CONCLUSION THAT C.C.P. SECTION 664.6 CONTROLS AND LACKS JURISDICTION TO DECIDE THIS ISSUE. THIS DOES NOT PRECLUDE ROSSI FROM FILING A SEPARATE ACTION TO ENFORCE THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM/AY) JUDGE: PATRICK J.
